# Testing Reference (Vue)

Use this reference when writing tests for Vue and Nuxt projects.
For general testing principles (what to test, what not to test, testing pyramid), see `references/testing.md`.
This file covers Vue-specific testing patterns.

---

## Setup

### Vitest + Vue Test Utils + Testing Library

```ts
// vitest.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config";
import vue from "@vitejs/plugin-vue";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue()],
  test: {
    environment: "jsdom",
    globals: true,
    setupFiles: ["./vitest.setup.ts"],
  },
});
```

```ts
// vitest.setup.ts
import { config } from "@vue/test-utils";
import { server } from "./mocks/server";

// MSW setup
beforeAll(() => server.listen({ onUnhandledRequest: "error" }));
afterEach(() => server.resetHandlers());
afterAll(() => server.close());

// Global stubs for Nuxt components (if testing Nuxt components outside Nuxt)
config.global.stubs = {
  NuxtLink: { template: '<a><slot /></a>' },
  ClientOnly: { template: '<slot />' },
};
```

---

## Component Testing with Testing Library

Testing Library for Vue works the same way as React — query by role, label, text. The only difference is the render call.

### Basic Component Test

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import UserCard from "./UserCard.vue";

test("displays user name and email", () => {
  render(UserCard, {
    props: {
      user: { id: "1", name: "Jane Doe", email: "jane@example.com" },
    },
  });

  expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { name: "Jane Doe" })).toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.getByText("jane@example.com")).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing User Interaction

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import SearchInput from "./SearchInput.vue";

test("filters the list when the user types", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();

  render(SearchInput, {
    props: { items: ["Apple", "Banana", "Cherry"] },
  });

  const input = screen.getByRole("searchbox", { name: "Search" });
  await user.type(input, "Ban");

  expect(screen.getByText("Banana")).toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.queryByText("Apple")).not.toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.queryByText("Cherry")).not.toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing Emits

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import ConfirmButton from "./ConfirmButton.vue";

test("emits confirm when clicked", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();

  const { emitted } = render(ConfirmButton, {
    props: { label: "Delete" },
  });

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Delete" }));

  expect(emitted()).toHaveProperty("confirm");
  expect(emitted().confirm).toHaveLength(1);
});
```

### Testing v-model

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import TextInput from "./TextInput.vue";

test("updates v-model on input", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();

  const { emitted } = render(TextInput, {
    props: {
      modelValue: "",
      "onUpdate:modelValue": (val: string) => {},
    },
  });

  await user.type(screen.getByRole("textbox"), "hello");

  const updates = emitted()["update:modelValue"];
  expect(updates[updates.length - 1]).toEqual(["hello"]);
});
```

### Testing Slots

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import Card from "./Card.vue";

test("renders header and default slots", () => {
  render(Card, {
    slots: {
      header: "<h2>Title</h2>",
      default: "<p>Content goes here</p>",
      footer: "<button>Save</button>",
    },
  });

  expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { name: "Title" })).toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.getByText("Content goes here")).toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Save" })).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

---

## Testing Forms

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import ContactForm from "./ContactForm.vue";

test("shows validation errors on empty submit", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();
  render(ContactForm);

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Send" }));

  expect(screen.getByRole("alert")).toHaveTextContent("Name is required");
  expect(screen.getByLabelText("Name")).toHaveAttribute("aria-invalid", "true");
});

test("submits with valid data", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();
  const { emitted } = render(ContactForm);

  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Name"), "Jane");
  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Email"), "jane@example.com");
  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Message"), "Hello!");
  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Send" }));

  expect(emitted().submit[0]).toEqual([
    { name: "Jane", email: "jane@example.com", message: "Hello!" },
  ]);
});
```

---

## Testing Async Behavior

### Loading → Data

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import UserProfile from "./UserProfile.vue";

test("shows loading state then data", async () => {
  render(UserProfile, {
    props: { userId: "123" },
  });

  // Loading
  expect(screen.getByRole("status")).toHaveTextContent("Loading");

  // Data loaded
  const heading = await screen.findByRole("heading", { name: "Jane Doe" });
  expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Loading → Error

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import { server } from "../mocks/server";
import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw";
import UserProfile from "./UserProfile.vue";

test("shows error when fetch fails", async () => {
  server.use(
    http.get("/api/users/:id", () => {
      return HttpResponse.json({ error: "Not found" }, { status: 404 });
    })
  );

  render(UserProfile, {
    props: { userId: "999" },
  });

  const alert = await screen.findByRole("alert");
  expect(alert).toHaveTextContent("Failed to load user");
  expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Try again" })).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

---

## API Mocking with MSW

MSW works identically in Vue and React — it intercepts at the network level.

```ts
// mocks/handlers.ts
import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw";

export const handlers = [
  http.get("/api/users", () => {
    return HttpResponse.json({
      data: [
        { id: "1", name: "Jane Doe", email: "jane@example.com" },
        { id: "2", name: "John Smith", email: "john@example.com" },
      ],
    });
  }),

  http.post("/api/users", async ({ request }) => {
    const body = await request.json();
    return HttpResponse.json({ data: { id: "3", ...body } }, { status: 201 });
  }),
];
```

Per-test overrides work the same way:
```ts
test("handles server error", async () => {
  server.use(
    http.get("/api/users", () => {
      return HttpResponse.json({ error: "Server Error" }, { status: 500 });
    })
  );

  render(UserList);

  const alert = await screen.findByRole("alert");
  expect(alert).toHaveTextContent("Failed to load users");
});
```

---

## Testing with Pinia

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import { createTestingPinia } from "@pinia/testing";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import DashboardPage from "./DashboardPage.vue";
import { useAuthStore } from "../stores/auth";

test("shows admin panel for admin users", () => {
  render(DashboardPage, {
    global: {
      plugins: [
        createTestingPinia({
          initialState: {
            auth: {
              user: { id: "1", name: "Jane", role: "admin" },
              status: "authenticated",
            },
          },
        }),
      ],
    },
  });

  expect(screen.getByRole("region", { name: "Admin panel" })).toBeInTheDocument();
});

test("hides admin panel for regular users", () => {
  render(DashboardPage, {
    global: {
      plugins: [
        createTestingPinia({
          initialState: {
            auth: {
              user: { id: "2", name: "John", role: "member" },
              status: "authenticated",
            },
          },
        }),
      ],
    },
  });

  expect(screen.queryByRole("region", { name: "Admin panel" })).not.toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing Pinia Actions

```ts
import { setActivePinia, createPinia } from "pinia";
import { useAuthStore } from "../stores/auth";

beforeEach(() => {
  setActivePinia(createPinia());
});

test("login sets user and status", async () => {
  const store = useAuthStore();

  expect(store.status).toBe("idle");

  await store.login({ email: "jane@example.com", password: "password" });

  expect(store.status).toBe("authenticated");
  expect(store.user?.email).toBe("jane@example.com");
  expect(store.isAuthenticated).toBe(true);
});

test("logout clears user", () => {
  const store = useAuthStore();
  store.user = { id: "1", name: "Jane", email: "jane@example.com", role: "admin" };
  store.status = "authenticated";

  store.logout();

  expect(store.user).toBeNull();
  expect(store.status).toBe("idle");
  expect(store.isAuthenticated).toBe(false);
});
```

---

## Testing Composables

```ts
import { ref } from "vue";
import { mount } from "@vue/test-utils";
import { useMediaQuery } from "../composables/useMediaQuery";

// Helper to test composables in isolation
function withSetup<T>(composable: () => T): [T, ReturnType<typeof mount>] {
  let result!: T;
  const wrapper = mount({
    setup() {
      result = composable();
      return {};
    },
    template: "<div />",
  });
  return [result, wrapper];
}

test("useCounter increments and decrements", () => {
  const [counter] = withSetup(() => useCounter(0));

  expect(counter.count.value).toBe(0);

  counter.increment();
  expect(counter.count.value).toBe(1);

  counter.decrement();
  expect(counter.count.value).toBe(0);
});
```

### Testing Composables That Use `useFetch`

```ts
import { mockNuxtImport } from "@nuxt/test-utils/runtime";

// Mock useFetch for unit tests
mockNuxtImport("useFetch", () => {
  return () => ({
    data: ref([{ id: "1", name: "Jane" }]),
    status: ref("success"),
    error: ref(null),
    refresh: vi.fn(),
  });
});
```

---

## Testing with Nuxt Test Utils

For integration testing Nuxt pages with full Nuxt context:

```ts
import { setup, $fetch, createPage } from "@nuxt/test-utils/e2e";
import { describe, test, expect } from "vitest";

describe("Users page", async () => {
  await setup({
    // Nuxt config for testing
  });

  test("renders the user list", async () => {
    const html = await $fetch("/users");
    expect(html).toContain("Users");
  });

  test("user can search (e2e)", async () => {
    const page = await createPage("/users");
    await page.getByRole("searchbox").fill("Jane");
    await expect(page.getByText("Jane Doe")).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText("John Smith")).not.toBeVisible();
    await page.close();
  });
});
```

---

## Testing Accessibility

```ts
import { render } from "@testing-library/vue";
import { axe, toHaveNoViolations } from "jest-axe";
import ContactForm from "./ContactForm.vue";

expect.extend(toHaveNoViolations);

test("form has no accessibility violations", async () => {
  const { container } = render(ContactForm);
  const results = await axe(container);
  expect(results).toHaveNoViolations();
});
```

Keyboard testing works the same as React — use `userEvent.tab()`, `userEvent.keyboard("{Escape}")`, etc.

---

## Test Data Factories

Same pattern as React — framework-agnostic.

```ts
function createUser(overrides: Partial<User> = {}): User {
  return {
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    name: "Jane Doe",
    email: "jane@example.com",
    role: "member",
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    ...overrides,
  };
}

const admin = createUser({ role: "admin" });
const users = Array.from({ length: 5 }, () => createUser());
```

---

## What to Mock in Vue Tests

| Keep Real | Mock |
|---|---|
| DOM rendering (Vue Test Utils / Testing Library) | Network requests (MSW) |
| User events (userEvent) | Nuxt composables (`useFetch`, `useRouter`) in unit tests |
| Component composition | Timers (`vi.useFakeTimers`) |
| Pinia stores (with `createTestingPinia`) | Browser APIs not in jsdom |
| Slot rendering | External services (analytics, logging) |
| Reactive state (`ref`, `computed`) | Date/time (for deterministic output) |
